description.en:
## Overview

The Golden Triangle marks the point where the borders of Thailand, Laos, and Myanmar converge at the confluence of the Mekong and Ruak rivers. Located in Chiang Saen district about 60 kilometers northeast of Chiang Rai, this remote corner of Southeast Asia was once the epicenter of the world's opium trade. Today it has been transformed into a peaceful tourist destination where visitors can stand on Thai soil and gaze across the river at two neighboring countries, take a boat ride on the Mekong, and learn about the region's fascinating and turbulent history.

## History

The name "Golden Triangle" was coined in the 1960s and refers to the vast mountainous area spanning Thailand, Laos, and Myanmar where opium poppy was cultivated on a massive scale. For decades this region produced the majority of the world's illicit opium. Thailand's royal family, particularly the Princess Mother through the Doi Tung Development Project, led crop-substitution programs that successfully replaced poppy fields with coffee, tea, and macadamia plantations. By the 2000s, opium production in the Thai portion of the Golden Triangle had been virtually eliminated, though it continues in parts of Myanmar.

## What to See

A large golden Buddha statue sits at the main viewpoint overlooking the river confluence, marking the exact spot where the three countries meet. The House of Opium is a small but informative museum right at the viewpoint area with an entrance fee of 50 THB, displaying opium pipes, weights, and photographs documenting the trade. The larger Hall of Opium, located about 10 kilometers away, offers a more comprehensive museum experience for 100 THB with interactive exhibits covering the full history of opium from ancient times to the modern drug war. Boat trips on the Mekong River can be arranged at the riverside for 300 to 500 THB, with some routes stopping briefly at a Laotian market on the opposite bank.

## Practical Info

The Golden Triangle viewpoint area is open during daylight hours and free to visit. The House of Opium is open from 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM daily. The best time to visit is during the cool season from November to February when the weather is pleasant and the river is calm. Getting here from Chiang Rai city takes about one hour by car or minivan. Most visitors combine the Golden Triangle with a stop at the ancient city of Chiang Saen, which has its own collection of temple ruins along the Mekong, and the nearby Doi Tung area for a full-day excursion from Chiang Rai.
description.nl: Historische samenvloeiing van Thailand Laos en Myanmar rivieren met opium museum

entry_fee: Free (viewpoint), 50 THB (House of Opium), 100 THB (Hall of Opium)
best_time_to_visit: November to February for pleasant weather; mornings for clearest river views
tips: Visit the House of Opium at the viewpoint for a quick 50 THB history lesson
Take a Mekong boat trip for 300-500 THB to see the three-country border from the water
Combine with a visit to ancient Chiang Saen ruins and Doi Tung for a full day trip
